{"key_event":"Northern Expedition and 1927 White Terror","reasoning":"The Northern Expedition and Nanjing government restored a more coherent central state than the warlord nadir, but authority remained uneven, fiscally limited, and constrained by militarists and foreign powers. Civil society reached a high point in strikes, boycotts, unions, and peasant associations, but the 1927 purges sharply reduced autonomous labor, Communist, and radical associational power by decade’s end.","society_change":-0.8,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":1.5,"state_power":4.7}
